description:
Umbrella epic for the work in `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md`, anchored to the **Practical backlog summary** as the grouping source of truth.

Use this epic when scoping any worker on the practical Mastra-parity effort for the single-session pi OM port.

Shared implementation context:
- Main backlog doc: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md`
- Timing rationale / key constraint: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/observation-timing-deviation.md`
- Package architecture: `packages/pi-om-extension/README.md` (How It Works, Lifecycle Hooks, Source Layout, Testing)
- Core runtime files: `src/index.ts`, `src/engine.ts`, `src/agents.ts`, `src/prompts.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `src/state.ts`, `src/config.ts`, `src/format.ts`
- Existing automated coverage: `packages/pi-om-extension/src/__tests__/{unit,integration,extension}`

Project constraints to preserve:
- single-session first; do not drift into thread/resource scope
- do not blindly port Mastra internals
- for continuous observation work, follow the agreed rule: observe continuously, publish discretely
- avoid mid-turn prompt swaps that would let pruning outrun injected memory

Definition of done for implementation tickets:
- update/add package tests in `packages/pi-om-extension/src/__tests__`
- run `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-om-extension`
- run `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-om-extension`
- run `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-om-extension`

Tier 2 epics intentionally remain blocked behind the Tier 1 foundations.

comments.text: Reworked the dependency graph to make `bw ready --scope sbdpi-f51` reflect actual delegation-safe concurrency instead of conceptual backlog groupings. The OM package has a very small shared core (`src/engine.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `src/state.ts`, `src/prompts.ts`, `src/format.ts`), so I tightened Tier 1 into a single serial implementation stream: staged/published state -> threshold split -> temporal anchors -> lifecycle markers -> observer specificity -> injected layout -> follow-on behavior -> regression lock-in. Tier 2 is also serialized behind Tier 1 completion, then current-task lifecycle -> gap/phase markers -> reflection robustness. Goal: avoid spinning up multiple workers that would mostly fight over the same prompt/state/rendering surfaces and create high-conflict rebases for little value.

title: Implement the practical Mastra-parity backlog for @packages/pi-om-extension
